meaning: adj.成格状的,有格子架支撑的
examples: Spanning the entire front of what is now called the Old Vine House, the grapevine was trellised and healthy.
What's more, on beams above and along the trellised corridor were 8000 painted scenes illustrating events from Chinese literature.
While in prison, Hunt transformed his cell in the prison infirmary into a bower of bliss with blinds, rose-trellised wallpaper, busts of poets, and flowers.
He's fond of square pools and ponds and trellised walkways leading from one garden area to another.
Similarly, Ivan opens the once formidable but now wilted towers, to reveal trellised bowers from which emerge brother heroes, one for each of the remaining 12 maidens.
